div1.entryFree:
Coin, vb., 1) to stamp, to mint: c. heaven's image in stamps that are forbid, Meas. II, 4, 45. let them c. his nose, H4A III, 3, 90. mightst have --ed me into gold, H5 II, 2, 98. the dog --s gold, Tim. II, 1, 6. Caes. IV, 3, 72. Cymb. V, 4, 23. Absolutely: they cannot touch me for --ing, Lr. IV, 6, 83 (Ff. crying).
2) to fabricate, in a good as well as bad sense: how many tales to please me hath she --ed, Pilgr. 93. whose gall --s slanders like a mint, Troil. I, 3, 193. so shall my lungs c. words till their decay against those measles, Cor. III, 1, 78. this gold must c. a stratagem, Tit. II, 3, 5. --ing plots, Cymb. II, 1, 64.
